response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced software developer with 15+ years across web, mobile, and embedded systems, but he is a poor match for this role on nearly every critical dimension. The most disqualifying factor is the absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, his background is overwhelmingly web-focused (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js), with only superficial exposure to C (National Instruments, a bachelor's-era project), no demonstrated Linux kernel internals work, and zero experience with the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or Layer 2/3 networking in a switch/embedded context.
strengths: 15+ years of professional experience across a wide range of environments including embedded systems, demonstrating strong adaptability
Some historical C/C++ exposure (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, bachelor's thesis audio project) and familiarity with Linux
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, code quality) that would transfer to any engineering role
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — the role explicitly requires native-level Japanese, making this a hard disqualifier
No relevant security technology experience: MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange, or attack/tamper detection are entirely absent from the resume
No embedded networking or switch firmware background; career is overwhelmingly web/cloud/SaaS with Node.js and TypeScript as the primary stack

key_factors.factor: No security or networking domain experience
